title: commit_sync hook: resolve real sha + make provenance append idempotent
source: 2026-05-26 session: observed while pushing the 11-ticket batch; commit_sync left B0003/B0032 perpetually re-dirtied after each commit.
why: Two defects in the backlog commit-sync PostToolUse hook. (1) SHA BUG: the dispatcher calls commit_sync with the literal arg HEAD and commit_sync does sha[:12], so every git_commit history entry records sha=HEAD instead of the real hash -- provenance is useless for tracing. (2) RE-DIRTY LOOP: commit_sync appends a history entry to every B-id MENTIONED IN THE COMMIT MESSAGE, post-commit and unconditionally. Any follow-up commit naming the same ticket re-appends and re-dirties the file -- a non-converging loop. Broke it manually by committing with a ticket-id-free message, but the hook should not require that workaround.
value_rationale: L (developer-experience / provenance hygiene; no pipeline or audit-correctness impact)
scope: Resolve HEAD to a real sha (git rev-parse) before recording, so extra.sha is a 12-hex-char hash, not the string HEAD. Fix in the dispatcher run_post or in commit_sync.git_show.
Make commit_sync.run idempotent: skip append_history when the latest git_commit entry for that B-id already carries the same resolved sha. Converges the re-dirty loop regardless of commit message.
Test: (a) recorded sha is 12 hex chars not HEAD; (b) running commit_sync twice on the same HEAD appends only one entry.
out_of_scope: Changing which commits trigger sync (still git-commit-only) or the B-id detection regex.
